text: Literally, this verse runs, ‘Or do you think that the passage in scripture means nothing when it says, The Spirit which dwells in you (in the Greek, which God has sent to dwell in you) longs after you even to envy?’ The whole phrase cannot be found in the Old Testament; the notion that God is a jealous God frequently occurs, e.g. Ex. 20.5. The Hebrew language does not distinguish between envy and jealousy.

‘The grace he gives us is something better still’; literally, ‘He gives a greater grace’, perhaps by way of contrast with the earthly subjects of petition mentioned in verse 3 above. The quotation is from Prov. 3.34.

It is difficult to see how this fits into the context, unless we suppose that the whole passage from 4.13 to 5.6 is directed against the rich, who are here accused of neglecting the great opportunities they have for doing good.
